title: On Masked path, check if /dev/null already mounted before mounting
body:
We do not want to mount /dev/null over a masked path, if the path is
already /dev/null.

This prevents an containers running buildah from requiring additional privs
to mount on a /dev/null, when the target is already mounted.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@nalind @giuseppe @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL

This fixes the issue we are seeing with people trying to run buildah within a locked down container with chroot.  SELinux is blocking the mounting of the second /dev/null on /proc/kcore.  /proc/kcore already has a /dev/null mounted on it.
Changes LGTM, test issues don't look to be related.

@vrothberg Not sure how I could write a test to test this.  I have tested this by hand though.

@rh-atomic-bot r=TomSweeneyRedHat
